jokes.text: You're nothing but a street rat! / I may be a street rat, but I still got your bread, bitch.
jokes.setup: Vendor calls Cricket a street rat as an insult
jokes.punchline: Cricket owns the label and flips it into a victory
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment - Cricket embracing his lowly status while maintaining defiance. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah, I'm the suck boy you're looking for. You want my time, you got to pay to spray. That's my motto. That and you cannot finish inside me.
jokes.setup: Someone approaches Cricket thinking he's a prostitute
jokes.punchline: Cricket's matter-of-fact delivery of his prostitution rules
jokes.explanation: Peak Cricket degradation delivered with professional pride. The motto format makes it extra absurd.

jokes.text: You're dying. / What? / I figured. You look like hell. Hey, has anyone claimed your organs?
jokes.setup: Dad says he wants to pass the torch
jokes.punchline: Cricket immediately assumes death and asks about organ harvesting
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - takes 'passing the torch' to its darkest logical conclusion. Shows Cricket's street mentality perfectly.

jokes.text: It's a seller's market right now.
jokes.setup: Discussion about organ harvesting
jokes.punchline: Cricket treats human organs like commodities with market analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - treating organ harvesting as a business opportunity. Dark humor with business terminology.

jokes.text: Do I look like a dusthead to you?
jokes.setup: Dad mentions Cricket smoking PCP
jokes.punchline: Cricket's indignant denial when he clearly looks like a drug addict
jokes.explanation: Classic denial joke - Cricket's lack of self-awareness about his obvious drug use.

jokes.text: In fact, I'm the type of gal who makes men's lives better. Hey-o! You guys mind if I shower in that leaky urinal again?
jokes.setup: Cricket claims he makes men's lives better
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks to shower in a urinal
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure. The immediate contradiction is brutal and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Well, that... don't count that. That's not a good example.
jokes.setup: Cricket just asked to shower in a urinal after claiming he helps people
jokes.punchline: Weak attempt to backtrack on the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the urinal joke - Cricket's pathetic attempt to maintain dignity.

jokes.text: Eh. While I'm here, I might as well... do a little P to the C to the P.
jokes.setup: Cricket is in the bar
jokes.punchline: Spelling out PCP like it's a cute acronym
jokes.explanation: Cricket's childlike way of referring to hard drugs - makes it both innocent and disturbing.

jokes.text: This is nice. I'm gonna sleep well tonight. / No, no. You're not gonna sleep there.
jokes.setup: Dad shows Cricket around the workplace
jokes.punchline: Cricket immediately tries to sleep in a shipping box
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cricket behavior - finding the worst possible sleeping arrangement and being excited about it.

jokes.text: Eh, too exposed. / No. I-I'll sleep in, uh, maybe the crawlspace or a cupboard.
jokes.setup: Dad says Cricket will sleep in a real bed
jokes.punchline: Cricket prefers increasingly confined spaces
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Cricket's complete aversion to normal comfort. The specificity of 'crawlspace or cupboard' is perfect.

jokes.text: When did I ever burn Dad? / You stole his identity. Eight times!
jokes.setup: Cricket asks when he ever burned his dad
jokes.punchline: Identity theft eight separate times
jokes.explanation: The specificity of 'eight times' makes it absurdly worse. Shows Cricket's selective memory.

jokes.text: Well, at least I didn't wet the bed till I was in high school. / Shut your mouth about that. / Whatever, Davy Diapers.
jokes.setup: Brothers arguing about past wrongdoings
jokes.punchline: Cricket reveals embarrassing childhood secret and creates nickname
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Cricket deflects with equally embarrassing counter-attack. 'Davy Diapers' is quotable.

jokes.text: His scalp came off on my fingers.
jokes.setup: Brothers fighting physically
jokes.punchline: Cricket's scalp literally comes off during the fight
jokes.explanation: Viscerally disturbing but perfectly Cricket. Shows his physical deterioration in the most uncomfortable way.

jokes.text: He's the one who stinks, by the way. His shoes smell like rotten eggs. / You smell his shoes?
jokes.setup: Cricket complains about Davy's smell
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actively smells Davy's shoes
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff - Cricket accidentally reveals weird behavior while trying to complain about someone else.

jokes.text: Yeah. He kicks at me if he catches me sleeping at work.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he smells Davy's shoes
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of sleeping at work and being kicked
jokes.explanation: Cricket's matter-of-fact delivery of being mistreated. Shows his normalized abuse.

jokes.text: I'm Belle. Thus the necklace my mother gave me that I'm currently guilt-wearing.
jokes.setup: Cricket asks her name
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily explains the necklace as guilt-wearing
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction - shows Belle's tendency to overshare personal details unnecessarily.

jokes.text: Hey, date! / Um, I'm sorry. What? / Shit. Sorry. Um, haven't done this in a long time.
jokes.setup: Cricket wants to ask Belle out
jokes.punchline: Blurts out 'date' as an opening line
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward Cricket moment - treating 'date' like a greeting. Shows his complete lack of social skills.

jokes.text: Damn it! How the hell did you guys find me? / A magician never reveals his tricks, Cricks. / Yeah. We did put that pet-tracking device in him.
jokes.setup: Mac says a magician never reveals tricks
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately reveals they chipped Cricket like a pet
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Mac's mysterious response immediately undercut by Charlie's bluntness. Shows their different personalities perfectly.

jokes.text: Wait. You did what? Wait. Did you put a chip in...? Did you put a chip in me?!
jokes.setup: Learning he's been chipped like a pet
jokes.punchline: Cricket's horrified realization building to a scream
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up reaction - Cricket's delayed horror at being treated like an animal.

jokes.text: All right. Fine. Three dollars. / Well, you don't go down in negotiation. / With Cricket, you do.
jokes.setup: Normal negotiation rules
jokes.punchline: Cricket is so worthless they negotiate DOWN
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of negotiation logic. Shows how little they value Cricket while being matter-of-fact about it.

jokes.text: Are we ready to party tonight? / So am I!
jokes.setup: Cricket high on PCP talking to his father
jokes.punchline: Dad enthusiastically agrees to party, not realizing Cricket is high
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - father-son bonding over Cricket's drug-induced enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Let's just have a good time and get drunk, huh? Stuff it down with some brown.
jokes.setup: Dad wants to bond with Cricket
jokes.punchline: Dad's own substance abuse philosophy
jokes.explanation: Shows where Cricket gets his problems - dad's own unhealthy coping mechanisms. 'Stuff it down with brown' is memorable.

jokes.text: No, you're not paying me in lemons again. / You want to get paid in PCP, Cricket?
jokes.setup: Cricket wants real money not lemons
jokes.punchline: Frank offers to pay in drugs instead
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the gang's exploitation - now offering drugs as payment. References past lemon payments.

jokes.text: Ah. I got to say, it's kind of fun being on the other end of it sometimes, huh?
jokes.setup: Usually they manipulate Cricket
jokes.punchline: Enjoying watching Cricket manipulate someone else for once
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on their usual dynamic with Cricket. Self-aware about their manipulative relationship.

jokes.text: Lemons.
jokes.setup: Cricket asking who has the money to pay him
jokes.punchline: Frank's single-word response revealing the absurd payment method
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic economy. Single word reveals the absurdity. Frank's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: My name is not Cricket, okay? My name is Matthew Mara. I'm a person. A person, by the way, who has a job now.
jokes.setup: Cricket trying to assert dignity
jokes.punchline: Desperately insisting on basic human recognition and his job
jokes.explanation: Tragic and funny - Cricket's pathetic attempt to reclaim dignity by stating basic facts about personhood.

jokes.text: Look, Cricket, I'm sure whatever street mongrel you're dating... she's into lemons, too, so you're good.
jokes.setup: Cricket protests he needs money for a date
jokes.punchline: Dennis assumes Cricket's date is also a 'street mongrel' who would accept lemons
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's complete disdain and assumption about Cricket's life

jokes.text: You guys are the worst. / He wants the lemons. / He needs the lemons for the scurvy.
jokes.setup: Cricket angrily taking the lemons
jokes.punchline: Charlie's medical justification for the fruit payment
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie logic - treating Cricket like a pirate who needs citrus for scurvy prevention.

jokes.text: And by the way, my name is not Cricket, okay? My name is Matthew Mara. I'm a person. A person, by the way, who has a job now.
jokes.setup: Cricket finally snapping at their treatment
jokes.punchline: His desperate assertion of basic human dignity and employment
jokes.explanation: Tragic and funny - Cricket having to assert his basic humanity. The repetition of 'a person' emphasizes his desperation.

jokes.text: He needs the lemons for the scurvy.
jokes.setup: They're discussing Cricket taking the lemons
jokes.punchline: Charlie suggests Cricket needs them for scurvy like an old-timey sailor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - applying historical nautical medical knowledge to modern homeless person

jokes.text: Look, I am not funny, and I'm sure as shit not cute.
jokes.setup: Cricket's suspicion about Belle's compliments
jokes.punchline: His aggressive self-deprecation and inability to accept kindness
jokes.explanation: Sad and funny - Cricket's damaged self-worth makes him reject genuine affection. Character-perfect response.

jokes.text: Are you thinking what I'm thinking? / Go behind some Dumpster and bang? / I was kind of talking about that date.
jokes.setup: Belle asks if they're thinking the same thing
jokes.punchline: Cricket's crude interpretation vs. Belle's romantic one
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Belle's romanticism and Cricket's crude street mentality. Shows their different perspectives.

jokes.text: I met the most amazing girl. I'm in love. I'm in love!
jokes.setup: Cricket returning to work excited
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top romantic enthusiasm after one interaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Cricket over-reaction. His desperation for connection makes him rush to love declarations.

jokes.text: Carl Lewis couldn't catch him! / I can.
jokes.setup: Davy says the client is too fast to catch
jokes.punchline: Cricket's confident declaration despite being a homeless drug addict
jokes.explanation: Great economy and confidence from Cricket. The Olympic sprinter reference makes his confidence even more absurd.

jokes.text: Did you just do a flip off that truck? / Yes, sir. That is how dedicated I am to keeping your business.
jokes.setup: Cricket apparently did a flip to catch up to the client
jokes.punchline: Cricket frames dangerous stunt as business dedication
jokes.explanation: Excellent Cricket moment - his street skills applied to business in the most extreme way possible.

jokes.text: Did you just do a flip off that truck?
jokes.setup: Cricket chasing the client
jokes.punchline: Client's amazed question about Cricket's parkour entrance
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Cricket's confidence. The visual of him doing parkour is both impressive and absurd.

jokes.text: Okay. Well, guess I'm gonna have to cut your heart out then. / What did you just say? / I said I guess I'm gonna have to cut... your heart... out of your body.
jokes.setup: Client refuses to reconsider business deal
jokes.punchline: Cricket threatens to literally cut his heart out, then repeats it for clarity
jokes.explanation: Peak Cricket - applying street violence to business negotiation. The clarification makes it even more threatening and absurd.

jokes.text: You see, uh, I'm from the streets. And I'm about to poke you full of holes.
jokes.setup: Cricket explaining his threat
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of his violent background and intentions
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to heart threat - Cricket's casual explanation of street violence as business strategy.

jokes.text: I said I guess I'm gonna have to cut... your heart... out of your body.
jokes.setup: Client asking him to repeat the threat
jokes.punchline: Cricket doubling down and adding anatomical specificity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous threat. The pause and anatomical clarification makes it more menacing and absurd.

jokes.text: He'll do it!
jokes.setup: Cricket just threatened the client
jokes.punchline: Davy immediately backs up the threat to save the deal
jokes.explanation: Good brother moment - Davy's willingness to enable Cricket's insanity for business

jokes.text: You see, uh, I'm from the streets. And I'm about to poke you full of holes.
jokes.setup: Cricket explaining his threat
jokes.punchline: Street credentials followed by colorful violence description
jokes.explanation: Cricket invoking his street background as business credentials. The hole-poking threat is vivid and cartoonish.

jokes.text: Sorry I said that about Carl Lewis.
jokes.setup: Davy reconciling with Cricket after success
jokes.punchline: Specifically apologizing for doubting Cricket could outrun an Olympic sprinter
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect economy. The specific apology for the absurd comparison is funny.

jokes.text: Jesus, Mary and Joseph, what are you doing?! / I'm making out with the woman I love. / That's Dad's dog. You're making out with my dog!
jokes.setup: Cricket thinks he's kissing Belle
jokes.punchline: He's actually making out with a dog
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and visual gag. Perfect Cricket degradation moment - confusing a dog for a romantic interest.

jokes.text: Dog? Oh, shit! Oh, no. Maybe it's time to make a change.
jokes.setup: Cricket realizes he was kissing a dog
jokes.punchline: Brief moment of self-reflection before giving up
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Cricket's brief attempt at self-awareness after rock bottom moment.

jokes.text: Nah, I'm gonna smoke it.
jokes.setup: Cricket considering making a change
jokes.punchline: Decides to smoke PCP instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cricket ending - moment of potential growth immediately abandoned for drugs. Great economy.

jokes.text: Maybe it's time to make a change. / Nah, I'm gonna smoke it.
jokes.setup: Cricket has a moment of potential self-reflection about changing his life
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides to smoke PCP instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cricket character moment - brief glimpse of self-awareness immediately abandoned for drugs. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: You can't go around doing insane shit like kissing dogs.
jokes.setup: Father is trying to give Cricket life advice
jokes.punchline: Has to specifically tell adult son not to kiss dogs
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of what should be obvious life advice but isn't for Cricket

jokes.text: You got to put all that nonsense behind you once and for all. / Hey-o! / Oh, Cricket! / Hey, you guys mind if I go in the bathroom and smoke some PCP?
jokes.setup: Dad giving Cricket ultimatum to change
jokes.punchline: Cricket immediately asks the gang if he can smoke PCP
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and irony - Cricket chooses the gang over family at the exact moment of the ultimatum. Great structural payoff.

jokes.text: Smoke up, bud. / Yeah, absolutely, dude. / We don't judge, Cricket, we don't judge.
jokes.setup: Cricket asking to smoke PCP
jokes.punchline: Gang's enthusiastic, non-judgmental support for his drug use
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast to his family's expectations. The gang's toxic positivity about his destructive behavior is peak dark comedy.
